summary: The classification policy is exceptionally consistent, rigorous, and highly conservative. All over-conversion guard and high-risk cases are correctly preserved as private holdout signal. Public regression candidate promotions are limited to safe, non-high-risk technical/domain gaps and baseline guard misses, requiring explicit maintainer verification.

findings.finding: Perfect structural consistency and policy compliance. Out of 52 total classified miss cases, all 29 over-conversion guards and 8 high-risk cases are correctly preserved under 'keep_as_holdout_signal'.

Case is correctly routed to 'requires_expected_recheck' due to 'possible_acceptable_variant' flag and 'semantic_term_variant_recheck' reason, complying with policy requiring maintainer review before updates.
Non-idempotent candidate gap case is correctly routed to 'move_to_public_regression_candidate' for maintainer analysis and removal from holdout before tuning.
Non-idempotent over-conversion guard case is correctly kept as private holdout signal, prioritizing security over functional tuning.
